setting.note: Allende never names the country, but its geography and its history are Chile's: the big house on the corner in the capital, the Tres Marías estate out in the countryside, and, in the last chapters, a very real September. The coup, the palace under fire and the stadium turned into a prison are Santiago in 1973. The map runs from the capital's streets north to the Aconcagua valley that stands in for the family's land.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Isabel Allende, first published 1982. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The House of the Spirits, Isabel Allende (1982). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of the family's spirits and their walled garden, the colour that runs through three generations of women. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
